free_text: Cross sections for (n,p) and (n,alpha) reactions with
14.5 MeV neutrons
A.W.R.E. 800 KV Cockcroft-Walton
Accelerator
500 keV deuterons on a tritiated titanium
target. 10**10 n/sec
In general about 100 mg of spectroscopically pure
material was used for each irradiation.
Samples for irradiation were sandwiched between two
thin aluminium discs and were placed close to the
back of the tritium target.
Activation analysis and
chemical separation.
4*Pi proportional counter and in 5 cases
end window Geiger counter with an Al entrance
foil, calibrated with a proportional counter.
used to monitor the
neutron flux
In order to calibrate the 24Na activity of the
aluminium discs in terms of neutron flux, a separate
experiment was performed in which aluminium discs were
irradiated in positions of known geometry with respect
to a tritium target. The neutron output from this
target was found by counting the associated alpha-
particles from the (d,t) reaction in a known geometry
and at an angle of 135 degr. to the beam.
Beta particles.
.Compared with predictions of direct interaction
theory.
.Self-absorption in beta counting negligible, contri-
butions from low energy neutrons negligible, also
contributions from (n,np) negligible.
Data from Tbl. on p.218 at PPS,73
The standard deviation errors quoted are due
mainly to:
* the counting statistics of the residual activity
the uncertainties in the neutron flux and
* chemical yield determinations
G.C.
N.Ch
